curl against the same throwaway PORT=8931 backend: first GET /api/board → 200 + ETag "76881daf…" over the 349617-byte body; refetch with matching If-None-Match → 304 Not Modified, 0 body bytes, same ETag echoed; refetch with stale If-None-Match "deadbeef" → full 200 with the 349617-byte body. Standard conditional-request HTTP, no poll-path special-casing.

Server-side reaping (tooth 5). Proven: @hono/node-server serve() with the timeouts passed via serverOptions AT CONSTRUCTION reaps a stalled/partial request with a 408 — 2.2s at a 500ms sweep, 29.9s at the production values (headersTimeout=20s, connectionsCheckingInterval=10s) incl. an upgrade listener mirroring injectWebSocket. Setting them post-serve() is INERT (sweep stays on 30s default). Active board-stream SSE STAYS OPEN past the window (requestTimeout bounds receiving a request, not the response). Raw-TCP supervisor proxy now reaps the peer on either-side close, truncation-safe via writableEnded. NOTE: the full spawned-child e2e is flaky to OBSERVE on the load-41 box (contended loop starves the unref'd sweep past 80s) — the incident's own condition; identical config reaps at ~30s in isolation.

REAL FINDING (flagged loud): slow-loris partial-header request NOT reaped at 90s on the real backend (child-direct AND public proxy). Isolated to a Node-runtime gap — headersTimeout & requestTimeout do NOT reap incomplete requests (reproduced on minimal http.createServer, Node 20/22/24), only keepAliveTimeout does. Backend config is correct; the platform reaper it depends on isn't firing, so abandoned-connection pileup protection is not delivered. Active-SSE-untouched half passes.

FIXED via socket-level reaper (reaper.ts installConnectionReaper). Root cause confirmed on Node 24: headersTimeout/requestTimeout don't reap incomplete requests via the sweep; only keepAliveTimeout fires. B verify on real backend (SPEXCODE_REAP_HEADER_MS=8000): slow-loris reaped at 8s deadline on BOTH public proxy and child-direct; concurrent idle /api/graph/stream SSE stayed connected the full 16s. Unit tests 3/3 + full suite 172/172 green.

REPRODUCED product-level: a slow-loris (TCP connect + partial headers) survives >70s on the real backend — child direct, supervisor proxy, AND the live main-checkout backend :8787 — so the failure pre-dates this branch. The reaper's own unit tests (7/7) pass on a plain node http/https server, so the mechanism is sound and the integration (installConnectionReaper on the @hono/node-server instance) is what's not reaping. The active-SSE half PASSES (stream held 35s stays open).

复测发现 main 上已存在的回归(issue #65):默认 30s 期限下 slow-loris 永不回收——边界二分锁定在 Node headersTimeout:20000(期限 15s 回收 / 25s 不回收);默认配置下防护实际关闭。SSE 豁免半边通过(held 45s 存活)。非本分支引入(main 对照复现)

A(fail), corrected diagnosis of #65: reaper never acts at default config — hidden serverOptions headersTimeout:20000 wins the race (m1: 408+close 28.7s child-direct) and silently caps the env knob (m3: SPEXCODE_REAP_HEADER_MS=40000 → Node 408 at 22.5s). Issue #65's '>70s never reaped' rows = deaf-probe artifact (ss: server side freed by ~30s; unread 408 blocks client-side close). No timer neutralization exists — reaper destroy lands fine when it fires first.

B(pass) @104f10b5: reaper single-owns deadlines after install zeroes headersTimeout/keepAliveTimeout + serverOptions deleted. Real backends: default config loris → bare destroy at 30.0s ZERO bytes (A: Node 408 at 28.7s); SPEXCODE_REAP_HEADER_MS=40000 → bare close at 40.0s (A: capped to Node 408 at 22.5s — knob now alive); /api/graph/stream held 45s stays open (1486B flowing). reaper.test.ts 8/8 incl. new #65 pin (hostile Node timeouts below reaper deadlines → still reaper-timed bare destroy); suite 231/231; tsc clean.
